slides.headline: "One-stop" is the most valuable status in sync licensing.

slides.body: Most composers know the term. Almost none fully understand how to get there — especially once a label is involved. If you control both → one-stop.
Here's the clear explanation. ↓ Supervisors strongly prefer one-stop. When time is short (it always is), they go there first.
ONE-STOP = you grant both licences from a single conversation.

LICENCE 1: The sync licence
→ Controls: use of your composition in visual media
→ Held by: the publisher — which is you, if you self-publish

LICENCE 2: The master licence
→ Controls: the specific recording
→ Held by: whoever owns the master — often a label, unless you recorded independently

slides.headline: Open briefs. Every Tuesday.
slides.body: Composers: apply once, get matched for life. Supervisors: shortlist in 48h.
